characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Badass Adorable: They’ve all stood up against ghosts to fight back against their tyranny (usually by chomping them), and they’re all pretty adorable little creatures.This especially applies to Pac-Baby, who is only a toddler. Badass Family:They might look like adorable sentient lemon drops, butthey’re not Pac-People to be trifled with, they’ve all gotten first hand experience chomping ghosts and traversing mazes. Even Pac-Baby and Chomp-Chomp know how to evade and attack ghosts. Battle Couple: Ms. Pac-Man and her husband have fought together multiple times against the ghosts. Big Eater: As one should expect with this franchise, they all love to eat cookies, fruits, and ghosts. The Bus Came Back: They slowly returned at a similar rate to their disappearance, with Professor Pac-Man returning inPac-Man Pop, Pac-Man Jr. and Pac-Baby returning inMinecraft, and the family being complete (albeit with some differences) inPac-Man Museum +.

characters.name: Pac-Man
characters.description: Pac-Man is the main protagonist of the series of the same name, and one of the most recognizable video game characters in the world. Action Dad: He has defeated the ghosts a thousand times before, and whenOrsontakes his children and his wife, leaving him to deal with it by dumb luck, he'll deal with ithard. Adventurer Outfit: He has a safari variant in theRe-Pacversion ofPac-Man World 2, and unlocks another safari-based variant inPac-Man Party Royaleby the end of the third world. Age Lift: HisGhostly Adventuresappearance, as well as games that use hisPac-Man Partydesign, have him depicted as a teenager, whereas in games that use his classic design (save for possiblyPac 'n Roll), he's a fully grown adult. Badass Adorable: A cute creature who can eat the hell outta the ghosts.

characters.name: Ms. Pac-Man / Pac-Mom
characters.description: Pac-Man's wife and the star of her very own arcade game. Action Girlfriend: She was one to Pac-Man inPac 'n Roll, andMs. Pac-Manprior to the game’s third act.. Action Mom: Ever since Act 3: Junior inMs. Pac-Man, she is the proud mother of Jr. Pac-Man, and at that point she's even more badass as she was on the Chase board. Adventurer Archaeologist: Mostly inMs. Pac-Man: Quest for the Golden Maze, where Ms. Pac-Man enters the "Temple of Dots" in search of the titular golden maze, exploring the inside while being chased by ghosts. Badass Adorable: Just like her husband, but evenmoreadorable.

characters.name: Pac-Man Jr. / Pac-Boy
characters.description: Mr. and Mrs. Pac-Man's son. He originally appearedbrieflyinMs. Pac-Manas a featureless infant before being properly introduced as a character inJr. Pac-Man. Adaptational Early Appearance: In the original release ofPac-Man World 2, Pac-Man Jr. only made a brief cameo in the ending sequence after Spooky was defeated (being the first Pac-Person to come out of hiding). In theRe-Pacversion, he first appears in the Pac-Village hub as an interactive NPC. Adaptational Seriousness: This occurs to an extent inPac In Time, in the PC versions of the game, Pac-Jr. ischeerfully smiling while with his familyand shows shock when his dad is zapped to the past. In the SNES version, Junior has aflat, almost stoic, expression the entire time during this scene. Ascended Extra:He only appeared during the third intermission ofMs. Pac-Manwith a minor role, he would later be given his own game and multiple recurring minor roles in thePac-Manfranchise.In the original release ofPac-Man World 2, Pac-Man Jr. only appeared in the game’s ending withthe rest of the crowd. In theRe-Pacrelease, Pac-Jr. (known as Pac-Boy) is an interactive NPC in Pac-Village and has multiple lines of dialogue alongside his sister. After Pac-Man beats Spookythe first timePac-Boy and Pac-Sisfall into the now glowing Golden Fruit Tree while investigating it, causing them two to go back in time andget captured by Spooky. This promotes him into a major character inthePost Game ContentwherePac-Manneeds to go back in time to save his children. He only appeared during the third intermission ofMs. Pac-Manwith a minor role, he would later be given his own game and multiple recurring minor roles in thePac-Manfranchise.

characters.name: Baby Pac-Man / Pac-Sis
characters.description: Mr. and Mrs. Pac-Man’s youngest child and infant daughter. Action Girl: Like the rest of her family she can navigate mazes, avoid ghosts, and chomp ghosts with as much versatility as the other members of her family. Advertised Extra: She was advertised by Namco quite often in the 80s and 90s despite never having many roles in the grand scheme of the series. Ambiguously Related: There’s another baby Pac-Person inPac-Man Arrangement+, but this infant has on a green double-pronged hat and a pacifier, rather than a red bow. Given that said character is playable along with Pac-Man and Ms. Pac-Man, the obvious implication would be that this is supposed to bethePac-Baby. Baby Talk: In the original version ofPac-Man Worldand the Re-Pac version ofPac-Man World 2 Re-Pac, Baby Pac-Man/Pac-Sis speaks in a manner reminiscent of an actual baby, using simplistic sentences and referring to Pac-Man as "Dada".

characters.name: Chomp-Chomp / Pac-Buddy
characters.description: Adaptation Dye-Job: Not initially asPac-Landreplicated his cartoon appearance, but most games afterwards portrayed Chomp-Chomp as somethingvaguely resembling a yellow beagle. Adaptational Intelligence: In the Hanna-Barbera cartoon, Chomp-Chompwas frequently shown to be a little on the slow side, often falling for schemes made by Sour Puss and occasionally disheveled the Pac-Man residence while being rowdy. Here, he’s about as intelligent as any other dog, but he’s more aware and competent. For example,Pac-Man Worldshows that he was able to go out and get Pac-Man a birthday present on his own, andPac-Man World 2displayed his heroism as he immediately went to get Power Pellets to chomp on the ghosts when they watching Pac-Village in the game’s ending. Canine Companion: He's very loyal to his master and helps him in the final battle against Toc-Man and going after the ghosts inWorld 2. Would have been emphasized in a planned localization ofPac & Palthat replaced Miru with him. Canon Immigrant: He was created for the Hanna-Barbera cartoon before becoming a member in the game universe inWorld. Cartoon Dog Breed: In most games he looks like a Pac-Man version of a beagle for coating, but it's hard to say what he is.

characters.name: Professor Pac
characters.description: Ambiguously Related: While the other Pac-Man family members take up more common family roles, his role is less common. The Japanese website forPac-n-Rollimplies he is the same person as Pac-Master, Pac-Girl's father, which in the modern day would make him Pac-Man's father-in-law and Baby Pac-Man and Junior’s grandfather. Divergent Character Evolution: Implied to just be Pac-Man as a teacher in hisfirst appearance,Pac-Man Worldwould establish him as a separate individual entirely (as well as giving him a mustache). Mentor Archetype: He teaches Pac-Man about the history of Pac-Land and how to stop Spooky. He may have also trained Pac-Man into fighting at a young age. Mortarboard of Intelligence: Wears a mortarboard on his head to show his intellect. Mr. Exposition: He plays this role inPac-Man World 2, explaining the history of Spooky and his defeat at the hands of Sir Pac-a-lot.

characters.name: Fairies
characters.description: A species of fairies that live in Fairy Land. Pac-Man helped some of them return to their mother. Ascended Extra: The species got several important playable individuals inPac-Man Monsters. The Bus Came Back:The Mother Fairy, having last been seen in the originalPac-Landin 1984, reappeared 30 years later as a summon in 2014'sPac-Man Monsters, as well as making cameos in 2014'sSuper Smash Bros. for Wii Uand 2018'sSuper Smash Bros. Ultimate.While they also made cameos in theSuper Smash Bros.games, fairies in thePac-Manseries had an 11-year gap between their appearance inPac-Man Monsters(2014) andPac-Man World 2 Re-PAC(2025). The Mother Fairy, having last been seen in the originalPac-Landin 1984, reappeared 30 years later as a summon in 2014'sPac-Man Monsters, as well as making cameos in 2014'sSuper Smash Bros. for Wii Uand 2018'sSuper Smash Bros. Ultimate. While they also made cameos in theSuper Smash Bros.games, fairies in thePac-Manseries had an 11-year gap between their appearance inPac-Man Monsters(2014) andPac-Man World 2 Re-PAC(2025).

characters.name: Miru
characters.description: The eponymous "pal" inPac & Pal. She’s a green girl who's a bit mischievous; while she doesn't harm Pac-Man, she grabs fruit and takes them back to the ghost house, which can be an occasional hindrance to him. Adaptation Species Change: Miru is said to be a ghost in some English material (mostly manuals for plug-&-play machines), despite looking quite different from the other ghosts. Japanese materialleaves her species unclear,though she was at least initially conceived as a ghost. Adhesive Bow Tie: Her bow just sticks to the top of her head without any problem. Aggressive Play Incentive: Letting her claim the bonus items counts towards completing the level, but you'll get less points. If you want the full score, you'll have to either snatch the item before she does or chase her down if she's already carrying it,especiallyif she's grabbed one of the items that lets you attack the ghosts. Artwork and Game Graphics Segregation: Looking at Miru's original sprite, it appears to be missing her character's hands and has white socks (presumably to communicate to the player that she's wearing shoes and walking). On a subtle note, she appears to have lighter-colored clothes than her artwork and appearance inMinecraft x Pac-Man.

characters.name: Blinky
characters.description: Adaptational Personality Change: As a result of his personality being switched with Clyde's, in the Hanna-Barbera cartoon he's cowardly and slow-witted, and isn't the leader of the ghosts. All Drummers Are Animals: The aggressive and ruthless Blinky turns out to begreatat playing the drums, asPac-Man World 2 Re-PAC's revised boss fight has him playing a giant set of drums (with the aid of an equally giant mechanical spider) for a live audience of fellow ghosts and Pac-Man. Animal Motifs: Whenever he combines himself with Kinky inPac-Man Arrangement (1996), Blinky becomes taller, bulkier, more aggressive, dons a large pair of horns atop of his head, and even gains a special attack where he blazes across the maze to ram himself headfirst into Pac-Man, which outright makes him a ghostly equivalent of a bull to go with his aggressive, stubborn personality. Ironically enough, he's red and bulls themselves aren't completely aggravated by the color red itself. Arch-Enemy:As the leader of the ghosts, he tends to be seen as Pac-Man's primary enemy. While not Pac-Man's most dangerous foe, he's the most common foe and the most aggressive in taking down the cookie muncher.He makes himself a personal enemy ofJr. Pac-ManandMs. Pac-Manin the former’s aforementioned game. He attempts tostalk, chase, and possibly kill Juniorthrough the game’s cutscenes for showing an interest in his daughter,Yum-Yum. He continues to attack the boy and chase him within the game’s mazes as well with his ghost gang in tow. This also causes Ms. Pac-Man to dislike him asshe’s wary of the threat he poses to her son, choosing to distract him in order to keep her son safe. As the leader of the ghosts, he tends to be seen as Pac-Man's primary enemy. While not Pac-Man's most dangerous foe, he's the most common foe and the most aggressive in taking down the cookie muncher.

characters.name: Pinky
characters.description: All Love Is Unrequited: Has a crush on Pac-Man inWorld 2, despite the fact that he's married. Animal Motif: Whenever she’s fused with Kinky, she gainsbunny earsand larger hands (almost resembling paws). Her special attack also has her leap around the stage in an attempt to stomp out Pac-Man. Bows for Cuteness: She’s occasionally worn bows to emphasize her cute and feminine side, most notably wearing a blue bowPac-Man World Rallyand a yellow/orange one inPac-Man Monstersfor some of her alternate variants. Breakout Villain: Pinky became playable inPac-Man Vs.andWorld 3, and was the most prominent ghost inGhostly Adventures. Characterization Marches On: Pinky’s infatuation with Pac-Man was oft seen rather disturbing and selfish inPac-Man World 2, other games and promotional material afterwards either omit thisVillainous Crushortreat it as more of a redeeming factor to her character.

characters.name: Inky
characters.description: Adaptational Dye-Job: In hisPac Man and the Ghostly Adventuresdesign, he's colored in a darker shade of blue, instead of cyan like in the games. Artificial Brilliance: By far the most complicated AI in the original Pac-Man, and this makes him the hardest ghost to track. The spot he targets is the middle point between Pac-Man and Blinky rotated 180 degrees around Pac-Man. Bumbling Henchman Duo: Inky sometimes partners with Clyde in a few games, when he does they’re usually shown to be cowardly, incompetent, buffoons who are much weaker than Blinky and Pinky. Child-Like Voice: In the Japanese version ofPac-Man World 2, his voice is high-pitched like that of a little girl’s despite being an adult ghost. Crosses over intoCreepy High-Pitched Voicedue to him trying to kill Pac-Man. Comically Cross-Eyed: InPac-LandandPac-Mania, his eyes are always cross-eyed to match with hisditzypersonality from the Hanna-Barbera cartoon.

characters.name: Clyde
characters.description: Adaptational Personality Change: As a result of his personality being switched with Blinky's in the Hanna-Barbera cartoon, he is the leader of the ghosts in several games. Affably Evil: Clyde is often the friendliest of the ghosts, he’s normally less focused on Pac-Man than the other three and tends to get the least violent, choosing to hide away if possible. Animal Motif: Clyde has a Frog affinity; inPac-Man Arrangement (1996), he has some frog-like qualities in how he looks (chubbier than usual with big cheeks and a long flat expression) and coughs up large Pac-Dots. In all versions ofPac-Man World 2, he operates his huge froggy mech that hops around, and has a tongue attack. Breakout Character: Became this in America where he was made the leader of the ghosts. Bumbling Henchman Duo: Clyde sometimes partners with Inky in a few games, when he does they’re usually shown to be cowardly, incompetent, buffoons who are much weaker than Blinky and Pinky.

characters.name: Sue
characters.description: Sue was an orange ghost that replaced Clyde in theMs. Pac-Mansub-series of games, she copied his AI patterns to the point beingnearly exactly like him. In most other titles, she is portrayed as amore dangerous purple ghost. Adaptational Dye-Job: She was originally orange inMs. Pac-Man, replacing Clyde. In the Hanna-Barbera cartoon she gained her trademark purple color. Arch-Enemy: To Ms. Pac-Man in theMs. Pac-Mangames. Canon Immigrant: She originated in the U.S. madeMs. Pac-Man, where she replaced Clyde as the orange ghost. She later appeared in the (Hanna-Barbera cartoon, where she gained her trademark purple color. She eventually appeared in official games, such asPac-ManiaandPac-Man 256. Commuting on a Bus: AfterMs. Pac-Man: Quest for the Golden Maze, Sue wouldn’t have another original appearance until a decade later inPac-Chainand would continue making infrequent appearances. In her appearances post-2010, she generally doesn’t have as major of a role as she once did prior, being treated more secondary than before.

characters.name: Tim
characters.description: The Bus Came Back: Reappears in the Pac-Man DLC forThe Sandbox Evolution. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: After his appearance inJr. Pac-Man, he wouldn’t be seen again untilThe Sandbox Evolutionin 2016, 33 years after his debut. He also has not appeared in a Pac-Man title since. Divergent Character Evolution:The Sandbox Evolutiongives him a peach-orange color along with a new behavior similar to Funky’s from256albeit with vertical movements instead. Odd Name Out: He took Clyde and Sue's place as this inPac-Man Jr. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: To Clyde, from the same behaviors to an extremely similar color scheme. He even reuses his design, though he would avert this inThe Sandbox Evolution.

characters.name: Yum-Yum
characters.description: Blinky's daughter whom Pac-Man's son has a crush on. Ambiguously Absent Parent: We never see Yum-Yum’s mother, her whereabouts aren’t stated. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Even more so thanTim, she hasn’t appeared any game sinceJr. Pac-Manand her last appearance was a cameo on the Namco Arcade website. Deuteragonist: Since the story ofJr. Pac-Manis about Junior and Yum-Yum’s budding romance, she would naturally become the second most important character after Junior himself. Flat Character: Not much is known about Yum-Yum besides her benevolence, her mutual attraction to Junior, and the fact that she’s Blinky daughter.

characters.name: Funky and Spunky
characters.description: Adapted Out: Funky’s scene on Ghost Island with Toc-Man is replaced by one with Inky instead inRe-Pac. Artifact Name: Funky’s former Japanese name, Common was to indicate that he was a common type of ghost, being mostly non-distinct compared to his contemporaries and referenced how multiple Funkys could be seen in the mazes ofPac-Mania. ThoughPac-Man Vs.andTaisen Pac-Mantreat him more as an individual with only one of him appearing, andbeing designated to a specific player slot(P4). Bald of Evil:Downplayed Trope. While he isn’t as evil as the main ghost gang, he was bald inPac-Man Tilt, while the other members had some type of hair on their heads. Brilliant, but Lazy: InPac-Man 256andThe Sandbox Evolution, Spunky normally spends her time dozing, being blissfully unaware of Pac-Man’s existence, but anytime she is shown awake she proves be formidable, she can jump as high as Pac-Man inPac-Maniaand her strategy for chasing down Pac in256is the same asBlinky’swhen awakened (she bolts after him). The Bus Came Back: After not being seen sincePac-Mania, Spunky would return inPac-Man 256.

characters.name: Kinky
characters.description: A gold-colored ghost that can empower the others. Cowardly Mooks: Unlike most of the ghosts that show up in the franchise, their frightened state is their default state, no Power Pellet required. Cowardly Yellow: As mentioned above, Kinky is quite cowardly, always vulnerable and squirming away from Pac-Man, and his default color (when not scared) is yellow. Dirty Coward: Kinky’sfartoo scared to face Pac-Man head on (evenInky and Clydecan be willing to get up close and personal with the yellow dot muncher) so he scurries away from him and heads to the nearest ghost so he can power them up. Fusion Dance: Kinky will seek out one of the four ghosts and merge with them.When combined with Blinky, the fused ghost gains aDash Attack.When combined with Pinky, the fused ghost can jumpfrom one end of the maze to another.When combined with Inky, the fused ghost can create a copy of itself.When combined with Clyde, the fused ghost leaves extra dots that count towards completing the level.
